name: WAV PCM to AIFF
summary: Zero-dependency streaming conversion of RIFF/WAVE integer PCM into classic AIFF.

scope: 8/16/24/32-bit integer containers and narrower valid-bit declarations
out_of_scope: IEEE floating point, compressed WAV and AIFC output
RF64/BW64 output larger than classic AIFF
Cue, broadcast, instrument and arbitrary application metadata migration

guarantees: PCM frame and channel order is unchanged
Eight-bit signedness and multi-byte endianness are converted deterministically
Declared valid sample bits are retained
AIFF 80-bit sample rates are encoded exactly for integer WAV sample rates
tradeoffs: Classic AIFF output is limited by 32-bit chunk sizes

properties: Generated AIFF is independently parsed before sample comparison
FORM and chunk sizes include required even-byte padding
Fragmented readers produce the same PCM sequence
regression: Extensible valid bits can compact a wider container
fmt chunks after data chunks are discovered
Multiple aligned data chunks concatenate in source order
fuzz: Planned: chunk graph, size arithmetic, extensible format and metadata fuzz campaigns
benchmarks: Planned: multi-channel and multi-gigabyte streaming corpus

limits: mapped metadata defaults to a 1 MiB limit
channels default to a maximum of 256
All sizes, offsets, frames and output limits use checked arithmetic
known_risks: Seek or output I/O errors may occur after the AIFF header is written
The static Adapter buffers forward-only protocol input to provide a seekable reader
